summary: First-finish smoke test of Bhupinder K. portfolio (backend FastAPI + React frontend). All 5 backend endpoints work with correct validation and CORS. Frontend renders all 10 sections with correct data-testids; theme toggle persists to localStorage; nav+mobile menu work; 4 project links point to correct Netlify URLs; contact form submit succeeds end-to-end (frontend->backend persistence verified) and shows success sonner toast + resets inputs. One minor gap: empty-form submission relies on HTML5 required validation instead of a sonner error toast.
passed: POST /api/contact valid payload -> 200 with id, name, email, subject, message, created_at
POST /api/contact invalid payload -> 422 (empty name, bad email, empty message)
GET /api/contact returns list including submitted messages
POST /api/analytics/track returns event with server timestamp, ip_hash, user_agent
GET /api/analytics/stats returns total_views, unique_sessions, total_contacts as numbers and reflects new events
All 10 sections render (site-navbar, hero, marquee, about, skills, projects, experience, testimonials, contact, footer)
Hero shows 'BHUPINDER.' and CTAs (hero-cta-projects, hero-cta-contact) visible
Theme toggle switches html.dark class and persists in localStorage after reload
4 project links have correct hrefs (ephemeral-choux-453feb, phenomenal-druid-8a63b1, r3buildingsolutions, webflow-page netlify domains)
Mobile viewport 375x800: mobile-menu-toggle opens mobile-menu with 5 mobile-nav-link-* items
Contact form submit shows success sonner toast 'Message sent...' and inputs reset
Submitted message appears in GET /api/contact (end-to-end integration)

failed.issue: Empty-fields submit does NOT display a sonner error toast; browser's native HTML5 'required' validation blocks submission instead, so toast_texts was empty in test.
failed.evidence: TOAST after empty submit: '' (empty string). Success toast fires correctly for valid submit.

action_items: Optional: If a sonner error toast is required per spec on empty submit, remove `required` attributes on inputs and add explicit client-side validation with toast.error(...) in Contact.jsx. Otherwise the native browser validation is acceptable UX.
